name: Aaron Rodgers
headline: A careful, low-ceiling streamer in his farewell lap
body:
The 2025 tape frames the bet cleanly: Rodgers finished QB15 with 226.1 fantasy points across 16 games, throwing 24 touchdowns against just seven picks but generating only 6.67 yards per attempt and a 42.3% success rate with a flat 0.02 EPA per play. He pushed deep on a mere 9.1% of throws and released the ball in 2.59 seconds, the profile of a quarterback emptying short and dodging contact rather than stretching the field. That efficiency floor caps the fantasy ceiling even when the volume holds.

At 42 and reportedly playing his final season, he reunites with Mike McCarthy and inherits modest help in Michael Pittman and rookie Germie Bernard. The 2024 QB8 finish shows what a healthier supporting cast once unlocked, but expectations have shrunk. He drafts near QB28 and is best left on the waiver wire as a superflex depth piece or matchup streamer, not a weekly starter.
